slice_name: Tag a client project with a label

composition.lld_notes: Framework: Django (carried from slice build-record lld_notes — not re-picked). Skeleton F1 routes + S4 F4 project routes inherited by reference — not edited (H14/B4/BF1). Feature-add wires the label capability into the EXISTING system at the declared INTEGRATION_SEAMS only (BF6): additive composition file web_ingress/project_label_dispatch.py (1) adds path('projects/<slug>/label') urlpattern + _view_set_project_label Django view (MPA/SSR, ADR-0004) routing into C6 ingress at the CT9 seam — a NEW additive route, no existing web_ingress code edited; (2) composes a NEW _ProjectLabelAdapter (does NOT edit the S4 _ProjectManagementAdapter) that reuses SessionResolver(CT3, existing C2) to gate the request + the SAME _PROJECT_DATA_STORE singleton; (3) calls C3 ProjectLabeler.set_label which persists the label field on the EXISTING project record via existing C1 at the CT2 seam (the aPRD INTEGRATION_SEAMS primary seam — CT2 EXTENSION, A14, no new contract, C1 internals untouched). existing_internals_modified=false — additive wiring only; the WSGI composition root grows additively (S5 route appended) exactly as it did for S4; no C1/C2/C3/C6 component internal logic edited. Synchronous, no async/queue (INV6).

mock_swaps.detail: C6 ingress dispatch to the label feature added via additive project_label_dispatch.py (_view_set_project_label + path('projects/<slug>/label')). No existing web_ingress component file edited (additive route only). Both C6 (prior-built, status:green) and C3 (extended, status:green) on path.

C3 resolves the freelancer session via existing C2 at the CT3 seam (reuses the baseline session pattern, BF5). real wiring composes existing C2 SessionResolver — C2 internals untouched. Both C3 (status:green) and C2 (prior-built, status:green) on path.

C3 set_label persists the label field on the EXISTING project record via existing C1 at the CT2 seam — the aPRD INTEGRATION_SEAMS primary seam (CT2 EXTENSION, A14: new label field on the existing project record, reuses the existing update surface, no new contract). real wiring delegates to existing C1 data store — C1 internals untouched. Both C3 (status:green) and C1 (prior-built, status:green) on path.

assertions.trace: _view_set_project_label routes projects/acme-website/label POST -> resolves session via mock_identity_auth.resolve_session({session_token:'sess-f5-valid'}) -> {'freelancer_id':'freelancer-42'}; calls ProjectLabeler.set_label('proj-1','freelancer-42',label='client-acme') -> delegates to mock_data_store.update_project with {'label':'client-acme'} -> returns labeled record; view returns {'status':200,...}; set_response['status']=200 in (200,201,302) = True

assertions.assert: any(p['id'] == 'proj-1' and p['label'] == 'client-acme' for p in list_response.get('projects', []))
assertions.trace: list view return_value projects=[{'id':'proj-1','label':'client-acme',...}]; the persisted label field renders on the project in the list; any(...) = True

second read after navigate-away: mock_data_store returns the same record {'id':'proj-1','label':'client-acme'}; label field still holds the value and stays linked to the same project (AC13); both equalities hold = True

failure.arrives_at: C1 raises StoreUnavailableError on label persist; C3 propagates unmodified (does not swallow); Web Ingress surfaces the persistence error as 503; the label is not persisted

assertions.trace: mock_data_store.update_project side_effect=StoreUnavailableError; ProjectLabeler.set_label propagates it unmodified (baseline propagate-not-catch idiom); dispatch_project_label_request except StoreUnavailableError -> {'status':503}; response['status']=503 in (500,503) = True

assertions.assert: not any(p.get('label') == 'client-acme' for p in list_after_failure)
assertions.trace: StoreUnavailableError raised before the record is written; subsequent list contains no project with label 'client-acme'; any(...) = False; not False = True

verification.static_trace_notes: No pytest runtime in build harness (Rule 6, §5.7). Static trace of all flow assertions: Happy path — _view_set_project_label resolves session via existing C2 (CT3), calls C3 ProjectLabeler.set_label which persists the label field via existing C1 (CT2 extension); set_response['status']=200 holds; label renders in the list (AC11) holds; label still holds the value on reload, linked to the same project (AC13) holds. Failure path — mock_data_store.update_project raises StoreUnavailableError; C3 propagates unmodified; dispatch surfaces {status:503}; response['status']=503 in (500,503) holds; label-not-persisted holds. All 5 assertions pass by construction against the wired path; the label feature wired into existing C6/C2/C1/C3 at the declared INTEGRATION_SEAMS via additive project_label_dispatch.py + a new adapter only — existing_internals_modified=false. Authoritative execution owed to VERIFY-OUTPUT (which also runs the scoped regression layer).

commits.message: INTEGRATE F5: wire project label into existing system at declared seams CT9/CT3/CT2 (additive label dispatch + session-gated persistence; existing internals untouched)
